title: Mildly Gooey Korean Soybean Sprouts Bowl
description: A cultured and gooey brunch dish featuring soybean sprouts, polished with a low-sodium friendly approach, originating from Korean cuisine
instructions: Bring the water to a boil in a large pot and blanch the soybean sprouts for 3 minutes.
Immediately transfer the blanched soybean sprouts to an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
In a small bowl, whisk together the gochujang, sesame oil, garlic, salt, and black pepper to make the sauce.
Once the soybean sprouts have cooled, drain them and place them in a bowl.
Drizzle the gochujang sauce over the soybean sprouts and toss to coat.
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve.
Let it sit for a few minutes to allow the sauce to seep into the soybean sprouts, creating a mildly gooey texture.
Serve the mildly gooey Korean soybean sprouts bowl chilled or at room temperature.
